characters.name: Aimee Godsey
characters.description: Aimee Louise GodseyBirthdayUnknownFamilyIke Godsey(adopted father),Corabeth Walton-Godsey(adopted mother), Unnamed Husband, Beth (daughter)OccupationStudentStatusAliveFirst appearanceThe Great Motorcycle RacePortrayed byRachel LongakerAimee Godseyis a character onThe Waltons. She is played by actress Rachel Longaker. She is the adopted daughter of Ike and Cora Beth Godsey. Initially, Aimee was very shy and apprehensive about being adopted. She would constantly hang out withElizabethand was afraid to live in the Godsey's store. Eventually, Aimee became use to life on Walton's Mountain and became very sweet and caring. Cora Beth would attempt to mold Aimee in her image, to the dismay of both Ike and Aimee. She is best friends withElizabeth. Initially, Aimee was very shy and apprehensive about being adopted. She would constantly hang out withElizabethand was afraid to live in the Godsey's store. Eventually, Aimee became use to life on Walton's Mountain and became very sweet and caring. Cora Beth would attempt to mold Aimee in her image, to the dismay of both Ike and Aimee. She is best friends withElizabeth.

characters.name: Chad Marshall
characters.description: Chad MarshallFamilyUnknownStatusAliveFirst appearanceThe CompetitionPortrayed byMichael O'KeefeChad Marshallis a young man that was very close to marryingErin Walton, and he also had strong feelings forMary-Ellen Walton. He has a cabin onWalton's Mountain. InThe Competition, Chad showed up onWalton's Mountain, and he was very dirty. He said he was doing a project for his school about trees. John Waltonthen invited Chad into his home, where he stayed for a few days.Mary-EllenandErincompeted for Chad's heart, whichErinwon. Chad was very kind and liked to tease people.
characters.personality_section: Chad was very kind and liked to tease people.

characters.name: Cindy Brunson Walton
characters.description: Cindy Walton(formerly Cindy Brunson) is the wife of Benjamin Walton, they were married in the Tv series and had a daughter Virginia also goes by Ginny she died from accidental drowning at a young age, they also had a son Charles he  was born onMothers Day on Waltons Mountainbut was never seen or mentioned again after that. Cindy is a very sweet and supportive wife to Ben, mother to Virginia and Charlie, sister to the others, and daughter to John and Olivia. She loves her whole family and would do anything for them. She made her final appearance inA Walton Thanksgiving Reunionand wasn't mentioned in the last two movies.

characters.name: Corabeth Walton-Godsey
characters.description:
When Corabeth first appears inSeason3' s"The Matchmakers", Corrie is shy, retiring, and acts as though she is downtrodden. She is rather plain, with her hair in a bun and simple, drab colored clothing. She develops, very quickly, after her marriage toIke, into what apparently was waiting to come out all along---a modern (1930s) prototype of Harriet Oleson (Little House on the Prairie) and a society debutante, with airs, graces, and ettiquette, as well as knowledge of the fine arts, not even seen among the Baldwin Sisters, the richest women in town. Corabeth often means well, but is interfering and gossipy in the affairs of the community, especially the Walton family, and is very pretentious, with fancier clothing and hats than most of the women can afford. Corabeth later tones down her outlandish behavior, after a drinking problem and some issues with her marriage, but remains pleasant but annoying down to the last episode. Cousin Sue Ellen s7/e15. 
Brother Albert is minister graduated Jerome College in Savannah s7/e15. 
Cousin Mary Margaret Fitz s7/e20. 
Uncle Lucas (mother's eldest brother) s8/e8. 
Operated Walton School of the Dance s7/e7 (also beauty parlor, hat store, tea parlor, real estate agent s9/e1) 
Sister Orma Lee s9/e14. 
Spinster Aunt Mitilda lives Doe Hill 5/9/82.

characters.name: Curtis Willard
characters.description: "I saw you kissing last Saturday night." "That didn't mean anything!" "I hope it did Mary Ellen, because I'm gonna marry you!" When Curtis first appeared inThe Weddingto replaceDoctor Vance, he was very rude and hard to get along with, as he told everyone harshly their problems with health.

characters.name: Ed Knightley
characters.description: Ed Knightleyis a minor character onThe Waltons. He is played by James O'Connell. His only appearances is inThe Test. He's a middleman contractor who hires John to make rocking chairs for a hotel, but when the hotel's boiler explodes, the hotel cancels the main contract and he has to cancel John's contract.

characters.name: Elizabeth Walton
characters.description: Elizabeth Walton, Portrayed by Kami Cotler  Elizabeth Tyler Walton (born October 1927), Elizabeth is the youngest daughter of the Walton family. Throughout the series, we see her grow from a very young child into a young woman. She is outspoken, very sensitive, and shares John-Boy's love and talent for reading and writing.

characters.name: Elizabeth Walton

characters.name: Erin Walton
characters.description:
Erin WaltonPhoto #1Photo #2Full NameErin Esther Walton-BridgesBirth NameErin Esther WaltonAdditional InformationGenderFemaleDate of BirthDecember 1921StatusAliveFamily / RelativesFatherJohn WaltonMotherOlivia WaltonBrother(s)John Walton Jr.,Jason Walton,Ben Walton,James Robert Walton,Joseph WaltonSister(s)Mary-Ellen Walton,Elizabeth WaltonSpouse[Jonathan Bridges](husband)Children5DaughterSusan Bridges,Amanda BridgesSonPeter Bridges[Jonathan Bridges Jr] [Zebulon Bridges]GrandparentsZebulon Walton(grandfather, deceased)Esther Walton(grandmother)RelativesCorabeth Walton-Godsey(cousin)Show InformationPortrayed byMary Elizabeth McDonoughCreated byEarl Hamner, Jr.First AppearanceThe Homecoming Esther Walton(grandmother) Erin Esther Waltonis one of the main characters onThe Waltons. She is portrayed byMary McDonoughShe is the second daughter and fifth child ofJohn Walton&Olivia Walton. In earlier seasons Erin was very close withMary-Ellen, the two would always been seen hanging out and/or gossiping. Erin was known for tattling on other people, which would often cause fights. Despite this flaw, she was very friendly, kind, and outgoing. Erin was always viewed as being extremely attractive, which became more apparent as she ages throughout the series. Right before Erin graduates High School, she becomes temporarily depressed, as she has no idea what she wants to do with her life (unlike her siblings such as Mary-Ellen's passion for nursing, John-Boy's passion for writing, or Jason's passion for music). She does, however, land a job working the Walton's Mountain telephone switchboard forFanny Tatum, Erin maintained a very flirtatious personality, and often hinted at wanting to pursue a relationship with Walton Mountain’s Minister, Reverend Henry (Hank) Buchanan. As this didn’t work out, She continued to date others before finding her last love interest of the Series. In later seasons, Erin became one of the most influential workers atJ.D. Pickett's metal products factory due to her standing up for women's rights in the workplace. InA Wedding on Walton's Mountain, Erin andJonathan Bridgesmarried; they have Five children: Susan, Amanda, and Peter and Jonathan Bridges Jr and Zebulon Bridges. Later on inA Walton Thanksgiving Reunion,  
|-|List of appearances=

characters.name: Ike Godsey
characters.actor_name: Woodrow Parfrey (The Homecoming)Joe Conley(Season 1-Season 9+ reunion movies)
characters.description: <tabber>General Information=Ike GodseyBirthdaySeptember 24, 1901FamilyCorabeth Walton Godsey(wife),Aimee  Godsey(Adopted daughter)StatusDeceasedFirst appearanceThe HomecomingPortrayed byWoodrow Parfrey (The Homecoming)Joe Conley(Season 1-Season 9+ reunion movies)Ike Godseyis a major character onThe Waltons. He is portrayed by Woodrow Parfrey inThe Homecomingand Joe Conely in the main series. He runs theGeneral Merchantile Storeand is responsible for maintaining stock, sending/receiving mail, taking phone calls, and tending to customers at the gas station. He eventually marriesCorabeth Walton-GodseyinThe Matchmakersand they adopt a daughter,Aimee, inThe Great Motorcycle Race. Ike is one of the friendliest neighbors onWalton's Mountainand a long time friend ofJohn. Like John, he is very hard working and loves his store dearly (inThe Attackhe states he would rather die at the register than in some rocking chair). He has his own organizational system and, until he becomes married, runs the store by himself. He loves to play pool with his friends while enjoying their good company and a bottle of the Baldwin's recipe. Ike, being at the hotspot of activity onWalton's Mountain, is very nosey. He purposely overhears telephone conversations and attempts to but into other folk's conversation. When he hears about John-Boy's novel, he constantly asks him whether he's mentioned in it, and even brings him a box filled with baby books and pictures so he'll write more. He has his own organizational system and, until he becomes married, runs the store by himself. He loves to play pool with his friends while enjoying their good company and a bottle of the Baldwin's recipe. Ike, being at the hotspot of activity onWalton's Mountain, is very nosey. He purposely overhears telephone conversations and attempts to but into other folk's conversation. When he hears about John-Boy's novel, he constantly asks him whether he's mentioned in it, and even brings him a box filled with baby books and pictures so he'll write more.

characters.name: J.D. Pickett
characters.description: Jefferson Davis Pickett, best known as J.D. runs a metal products factory that Erin Walton works.

characters.name: James Robert Walton
characters.description: James Robert WaltonGenderMaleBirthdayJune 13, 1924FamilyZebulon Walton(Grandfather),Esther Walton(Grandmother),Benjamin Walton(uncle),John Walton(Father),Olivia Walton(Mother),John Jr.Walton,Jason Walton,Ben Walton,Joseph Walton(brothers),Mary-Ellen Walton,Erin Walton,Elizabeth Walton(sisters)OccupationPilotStatusAliveFirst appearanceThe FoundlingPortrayed byDavid W. HarperJames Robert "Jim-Bob" Walton (born June 13, 1924)is the youngest Walton boy, and the 6th child ofOlivia WaltonandJohn Walton. His twin,Joseph Walton, died at birth. James Robert is referred to as "Jim-Bob" and, although he tries to shake it off as he gets older, the nickname sticks.  Always fascinated with aviation (The Air Mail Man), in later episodes he wants joins the Air Force as soon as he can and even has his own avaiation helmet. Jim-Bob built a car from scratch, and it runs decently, although it does have occasional problems. He gets in a lot of fights withBen. In the episodeThe Home Front, he dates adoptive cousinAimee Godsey. Jim-Bob is sort of a space cadet, he's always got his head in the clouds dreaming about the future, which sometimes causes him to slack off in his chores. He usually refers to scenarios as being "dumb". Jim-Bob has a fascination with anything mechanical, especially cars, building his own from scratch with assorted scrap metal found in the junkyard. He has a passion for aviation and wants to become an airplane pilot in the Air Force.

He has a passion for aviation and wants to become an airplane pilot in the Air Force.

characters.name: James Robert Walton
characters.description: James Robert WaltonGenderMaleBirthdayJune 13, 1924FamilyZebulon Walton(Grandfather),Esther Walton(Grandmother),Benjamin Walton(uncle),John Walton(Father),Olivia Walton(Mother),John Jr.Walton,Jason Walton,Ben Walton,Joseph Walton(brothers),Mary-Ellen Walton,Erin Walton,Elizabeth Walton(sisters)OccupationPilotStatusAliveFirst appearanceThe FoundlingPortrayed byDavid W. HarperJames Robert "Jim-Bob" Walton (born June 13, 1924)is the youngest Walton boy, and the 6th child ofOlivia WaltonandJohn Walton. His twin,Joseph Walton, died at birth. James Robert is referred to as "Jim-Bob" and, although he tries to shake it off as he gets older, the nickname sticks.  Always fascinated with aviation (The Air Mail Man), in later episodes he wants joins the Air Force as soon as he can and even has his own avaiation helmet. Jim-Bob built a car from scratch, and it runs decently, although it does have occasional problems. He gets in a lot of fights withBen. In the episodeThe Home Front, he dates adoptive cousinAimee Godsey. Jim-Bob is sort of a space cadet, he's always got his head in the clouds dreaming about the future, which sometimes causes him to slack off in his chores. He usually refers to scenarios as being "dumb". Jim-Bob has a fascination with anything mechanical, especially cars, building his own from scratch with assorted scrap metal found in the junkyard. He has a passion for aviation and wants to become an airplane pilot in the Air Force.

He has a passion for aviation and wants to become an airplane pilot in the Air Force.

characters.name: Jenny Pendleton
characters.description: Jenny Pendleton(first appeared inThe Love Storyand last appeared inThe Thanksgiving Story), wasJohn-Boy'ssecond girlfriend. They both had very strong feelings for each other, andJohn-Boyeven says inThe Elopementthat he really wanted to marry Jenny. Jenny's father wasDave Pendleton, and her mother died when she was very little.Daveand Jenny then moved away, but returned about seventeen years later. This time,Davehas a wife,Eula Pendleton. Jenny always lovedJohn-Boy'swriting, and was very optimistic and fun.
characters.personality_section: Jenny always lovedJohn-Boy'swriting, and was very optimistic and fun.

characters.name: John Walton
characters.actor_name: Andrew Duggan(The Homecoming)Ralph Waite(Season 1-Season 9+ all reunion movies)
characters.description: John WaltonGenderMaleBirthday1896FamilyZebulon Walton(father),Esther Walton(mother),Olivia Walton(wife),John-Boy Walton,Jason Walton,Ben Walton,Jim-Bob Walton,Joseph Walton(sons),Mary-Ellen Walton,Erin Walton,Elizabeth Walton(daughters)StatusAliveFirst appearanceThe HomecomingPortrayed byAndrew Duggan(The Homecoming)Ralph Waite(Season 1-Season 9+ all reunion movies)John Waltonis one of the main characters onThe Waltons. He is the son ofZebulon Walton&Esther Walton, brother of the lateBenjamin Walton, married toOliviaand the father ofJohn-Boy,Jason,Mary-Ellen,Ben,Erin,Jim-BobandElizabeth. Although he looks gruff on the surface, he has an extremely deep love for his family andWalton's Mountainand gets defensive if either one becomes threatened. Despite this, John is relatively laid back and usually let's his children make decisions for themselves, much to Olivia's chagrin. He pushes his children to pursue whatever they want, even if it's not what he wanted for them. John is extremely hard working and believes jobs should be finished once they are started, and does not want any of his children goofing off when there is work to be done. John is not as religious as the rest of family, and he usually stays home from church on Sundays, only attending for special occasions.

characters.name: John Walton Jr.
characters.description: Name:John Walton Jr.Nickname:John-BoyBirthday:1916First appearance:The HomecomingPortrayed By:Richard Thomas (Seasons 1-6) Robert Wightman (8-9) Johnathan Lee Walton Jr.is the main character inThe Waltons. The series is told through his perspective growing up onWalton's Mountain. The beginning and ending of each episode are excerpts from John-Boy's journal. Throughout the series, he is commonly referred to as ''John-Boy." John Lee Walton Jr.is the oldest child ofJohn WaltonandOlivia Walton. He has three brothers;Jason Walton,James Robert "Jim-Bob" Walton,Ben Waltonand three sisters;Mary-Ellen Walton,Erin Walton, andElizabeth Walton. He grew up in the Blue Ridge Mountains known asWalton's Mountain. He is very family-oriented and has a special relationship with his brothers and sisters. He is very polite, courteous, and friendly. He can also be very talkative, stubborn and independent. He will stand up for what is right and tends to get very emotional while talking.
characters.personality_section: He is very family-oriented and has a special relationship with his brothers and sisters. He is very polite, courteous, and friendly.
